item.title: 320 | Robbe Reddinger, Believe in the Run | Origin Stories
item.description:
Feels weird to write this about myself, but Robbe is co-host of The Drop running podcast, director of website affairs, editor in chief of Believe in the Run, father of raccoons, urban redneck, hot and cold runner, music maker and dreamer of dreams. This is his story, his history, from beginning to end (with honestly, a lot left out). It's been a wild ride, and thanks to all of you for coming along– it's been the honor of a lifetime to know you and I forever indebted to all of you for allowing me to do this with my life.


Follow Robbe on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/normcorerunner/

item.title: 318 | Taylor Bodin, BITR Lead Trail Reviewer
item.description:
Get to know the head of trail running here at Believe in the Run, Taylor Bodin. A Minnesota native, Taylor joins the ranks of legends like Courtney Dauwalter and Scott Jurek who have left the land of a thousand lakes for the mountains of 14,000 feet. Over the past five years, Taylor has reviewed hundreds of trail running shoes, and will be lined up for even more as he joins the team full time in 2025.

Follow Taylor on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/taylorjbodin/

item.title: 316 | Scott Jurek, Ultrarunning God (Live from The Running Event w/ Satisfy)
item.description:
Coming at you live from the floor of The Running Event (hence the audio) from inside the Satisfy booth, Thomas and Robbe chat with ultrarunning legend and Possessed Magazine cover boy Scott Jurek about growing up in Minnesota, fishing, transitioning into different stages of running and life, and how to not break your ankles while running on the pile of rocks known as the Pennsylvania section of the Appalachian Trail. We also somehow forgot to ask him about @yaboyscottjurek (not his account), which was our only regret.


Follow the real Scott Jurek on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/scottjurek/
Check out Satisfy Running: https://satisfyrunning.com/

item.title: 315 | The Running Event Recap: Highs and Lows and In-Betweens of 2025
item.description:
We have endured the long odyssey known as The Running Event, and have returned to you with treasures galore, i.e. information on what's hot and what's not for 2025. It was a nonstop ride with over 25 meetings in 48 hours to see what's coming, and we try to give you our best analysis, in between getting sidetracked. Also, Meg may have been trafficked without knowing it. Guess we'll find out when the court docs are unsealed!
Registration for Winter GRIT: Arctic Adventure opens at 9 a.m. ET on Black Friday (11/29). It will sell out on the same day, so make sure you set your alarm and register here: https://www.believeintherun.com/grit

item.itunes:summary:
We have endured the long odyssey known as The Running Event, and have returned to you with treasures galore, i.e. information on what's hot and what's not for 2025. It was a nonstop ride with over 25 meetings in 48 hours to see what's coming, and we try to give you our best analysis, in between getting sidetracked. Also, Meg may have been trafficked without knowing it. Guess we'll find out when the court docs are unsealed!

item.title: 313 | Nike Pegasus Premium / On Cloudboom Strike LS
item.description:
Meg is out, Karl is in, for this episode of The Drop, which means it's just chaos without our guardrails in place. From our thoughts on homesteading and van life to mid-run conversations with strangers to the horror cinema classic Tales From the Hood, we cover all the unimportant things in live before finally getting around to the On Cloudboom LS and Nike Pegasus Premium.

item.title: 311 | Nike Zoom Fly 6 / Asics Novablast 5 Double Feature + Mindless Insanity
item.description:

Believe it or not, we eventually get around to the Nike Zoom Fly 6 and Asics Novablast 5.

item.title: 310 | Charles Wesley Godwin, Musician
item.description:
A wild and wonderful songwriter from the great state of West Virginia, Charles Wesley Godwin is also a passionate runner who incorporates it into his life both on and off the road. In this episode, Robbe chats with him about music, touring, Venus fly traps, baseball, The Avett Brothers, and being a Mountaineer. This was just a really great conversation that probably could've gone on for hours; if you're a fan of music, running, and/or life, you're going to enjoy this one.
Join his Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/CharlesWesleyG

Follow Charles on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/charleswgodwin/


Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/artist/2ErsJAz6qJ5cqjoVAvfvaC


Check out his tour dates: https://www.charleswgodwin.com/

item.title: 309 | NYC Marathon Recap
item.description:
We took the train to New York City and Meg and Karl ran a race with 50,000 friends. Needless to say, it was a pretty fun weekend and we got to share it with a whole bunch of you. Here's our recap, with plenty of side tangents along the way. And yes, we talk about Matt Choi (but recorded this episode before he gave his apology). Can't think of anything more dramatic that happened this past week...

item.title: 308 | Julie St-Louis, Ciele Athletics
item.description:
As headwear designer for Montreal-based Ciele Athletics, Julie St-Louis is responsible for making you look and feel great for training and race day. She's behind some of the iconic styles from Ciele, with plenty more in store. We get her behind-the-scenes look at the apparel industry and her processes and inspirations for creating utilitarian apparel designs.
Check out Ciele: https://cieleathletics.com/

item.title: 307 | Diadora Nucleo 2, Lego Brick Builders, Car Talk, Amsterdam Report
item.description:
We're in that floating period of fall marathons where some of us are tapering while others are taking a running sabbatical. No worries, we find a way to fill the space with Lego talk and Ryan's post-Amsterdam Marathon race report in addition to a myriad of other topics that has Meg descending into madness. But at least her Ultra Human Air ring can tell her whether she's losing her mind or staying healthy and sane.

item.title: 306 | Rhythm in the Darkness
item.description:
We love music and we love ultrarunning and this episode is a perfect intersection of those two things. From touring with Katy Perry and playing for Kelly Clarkson to starring on this season of The Voice, Kyle Whalum, Brad Sample, and Caleb Gilbreath are friends, professional musicians, and self-proclaimed running nerds who set out to train for, and complete the Pinhoti 100 ultramarathon on top of their already chaotic lives as they struggle to balance family life with the chaos of the music industry. Rhythm in the Darkness is a documentary about all of that and more, and so is today's conversation. It's a fun one for sure, so make sure you check out the documentary and the corresponding album at the links below.
Watch Rhythm in the Darkness on YouTube: https://youtu.be/5pTUp-Pz05wListen to DFL Music's "Rhythm in the Darkness" EP: https://open.spotify.com/album/4YHJe49STFwNqLway8BA3P

item.title: 303 | Chicago Marathon Recap, Bagel Talk, Deep Dish Slander
item.description:
Look, everything in Chicago was great, especially the Chicago Marathon and the big group of running raccoons wearing Believe singlets as they chased down their goals over 26.2 miles. The epic shakeout run was also pretty rad. But we're just saying that maybe deep dish isn't for us and that tavern pizza is mid. The dogs ruled, though. Unsurprisingly, we manage to talk mostly not about the marathon, but eventually we get around to it.


Thanks to MarathonFoto and New Balance and Endorphins Running and Fleet Feet for a special weekend, let's run it back again.

item.title: 302 | Kilian Jornet, The Greatest Trail Runner and Ski Mountaineer of All Time
item.description:
No matter what his Wikipedia entry says, everyone in the world knows that Kilian Jornet is the greatest. We were so lucky to interview this legend about all his life's adventures, including his most recent feat with the Alpine Connections, in which he traversed all 82 peaks of 4,000 meters or more in the Alps, doing it in 19 days while covering over 220,000 feet in elevation gain. We talk about that endeavor, growing up in the mountains, raising kids to be independent, and so much more. Thanks to Coros for this opportunity and to Kilian for sharing his story.

Learn more about the Alpine Connections project: https://coros.com/stories/more-than-splits/c/alpine-connections

item.title: 299 | Berlin Marathon Recap
item.description:
They say to give gold on the 50th anniversary, but we decided to get it instead. This is our recap of the 50th Berlin Marathon, a weekend filled with everything that makes running golden: community events, shoe releases, friends gained along the way, hardships and victories. We came away with a deeper appreciation and respect and gratitude for all it is we get to do, none of which would be possible without all of you. Thank you Berlin, and thank you believers. item.dc:creator: Believe in the Run
item.itunes:summary:
They say to give gold on the 50th anniversary, but we decided to get it instead. This is our recap of the 50th Berlin Marathon, a weekend filled with everything that makes running golden: community events, shoe releases, friends gained along the way, hardships and victories. We came away with a deeper appreciation and respect and gratitude for all it is we get to do, none of which would be possible without all of you. Thank you Berlin, and thank you believers. SUPPORT OUR SPONSORS!

LMNT Our go-to supplement for before, during, and after the Berlin Marathon, there is just nothing out there that packs the sodium and electrolytes like LMNT (and it tastes great, too). Pick up your LMNT and get a free sample pack with your order here ➡ ⁠http://drinklmnt.com/thedrop⁠ CHERIBUNDI Cherbundi’s tart cherry juice helps reduce inflammation, improve sleep, and aid in faster recovery. Shop here: https://cheribundi.com/collections/shop/products/pure-gummies

item.title: 298 | Carson Caprara, Brooks' Senior Vice President of Footwear
item.description: With some great updates to the Hyperion line and the release of the Brooks Glycerin Max, we think it's safe to say that Brooks is back. Today's guest, Carson Caprara, had a big hand in that as he oversaw the efforts in footwear over the past couple years, and the fruits of that labor are starting to pay off. None of us hold anything back as we talk about the state of Brooks, navigating the pandemic, revving up on innovation, and what the future holds for the #1 brand in running. Just another great episode for all the industry-curious listeners out there.

item.description:
Sport psychology coach Emily Saul takes us through a pre-marathon meditation, just in time for all our fall marathons. Listen the night before to relax and get in the right mindset.


Follow Emily on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/emilysaulboston/?hl=en

item.title: 297 | Adidas Adizero Adios Pro 4, Ear Fears, Nike's New CEO
item.description:
The Berlin Marathon is just a couple days, and technically we're already there, but we're still back in Baltimore for this episode of the podcast. We keep it weird with our running updates and our discussion on Thomas and Robbe's shared fear of ears, while throwing in some industry talk on account of Nike's shakeup in leadership. We also give some initial thoughts on the Adidas Adios Pro 4.

item.title: 296 | Patrick Nava, Global Vice President of Product Management Running at Adidas
item.description: Thomas and Robbe sit down and chat with Patrick Nava, Global Vice President of Product Management Running at Adidas, about all things running at Adidas, including everything that went into the development of the all-new Adios Pro 4 and the Evo Pro 1. Another good one for all the shoe geeks out there.

item.title: 295 | Brooks Glycerin Max, Last Long Runs, Soft Pretzels Need More Air Time
item.description:
This week on the podcast, shoe reviewer Alex joins us to talk about his trip to Austria and the Adidas Terrex Infinite Trails 46K race. Then we dive into our own running, along with spectating cross-country, a deep-dive on soft pretzels, how to make a special Dr. Pepper beverage, and the adventures of Robbe’s 20-miler. Finally, we discuss the Brooks Glycerin Max, which is probably our favorite shoe from Brooks, ever.

BERLIN MARATHON GROUP RUN W/ ADIDAS

We finally have some Berlin details, so listen up! Save the date for Friday, 9/27 at 8 a.m. Meet us at the adidas Berlin Sport Base for a shakeout run and so much more. T-shirt giveaway (seriously it's a good one) to the first 150 runners who show up. We will run a 5K distance (with adidas product testing of course), followed by food and music and guest appearances. It's going to be huge, you DO NOT WANT TO MISS IT.


SUPPORT OUR SPONSORS!

item.title: 294 | Paul Lang, ASICS Global Senior Product Manager
item.description: On this special in-studio episode of The Drop, Thomas and Meg chat with Paul Lang, ASICS global senior product manager. Go behind the scenes of what it's like to work for one of the biggest brands in running, how development and supply chains work, and what it's like figuring out consumer wants and buyer demands. Definitely a good one for all the industry nerds out there.

item.title: 293 | Berlin Tune-Up Race, Fila Has a Good Running Shoe, Multilingual Dogs
item.description:
In this episode, we recap our weekend performances at the Charles Street 12-Miler, our de facto tune-up race for Berlin. Meg crushed it, Robbe barely made it, Karl also showed up big, and Thomas– well, he's back, folks (mostly). We also talk about some new Diadora gear and the Fila Float Maxxi 2 Pro, a Brazilian model that's kinda hot... for a shoe.

item.title: 292 | Keira D'Amato, Nike Pro Athlete
item.description:
Keira's back! Our favorite pro athlete/Oreo reviewer/costume expert extraordinaire is back to chat all things running, training, and moving to Utah. This, after her recent win at the U.S. 20K Championships. Just a legend doing legendary things.

item.title: 291 | Our Favorite Max Cushion Shoes, Curse of Thomas, Paralympics, Tip Culture
item.description:
We figured this week is as good as any to give you our favorite max cushion shoes of the year, but before we do, we talk about the curse on Thomas and how to break it, Keira D'Amato's 20K championship win, UTMB, and seriously– people need to recognize the greatness of the Paralympics.

item.title: 290 | Brad Kroll & Conor Gay, MarathonFoto
item.description: You run a race, you finish, and then it's a memory. If it's a goal race, you probably want to remember that memory, and that's where MarathonFoto comes in. So much goes into getting you those photos, so we dive in and ask Brad and Conor how the race day orchestration works. By the end of this convo you'll have a much deeper appreciation for everything that goes into getting you that photo finish. Looking to run sub-4 at the Chicago Marathon? Run with Robbe and you'll have a 50/50 chance. Fill out this form and we'll put you on the list (please only fill out if you're actually running the race): https://forms.gle/vXRtBQ3edezFEEGu5

item.title: 289 | Adidas Supernova Prima, Champagne Supernova, Stuck-in-Space Supernova
item.description:
It's a whole lot of supernova talk on today's episode, with both the Adidas Supernova Prima and the Oasis reunion and what goes on when you're stuck in space for eight months. We also get Thomas's take on Alien: Romulus, and Robbe goes full Encyclopedia Brown in solving The Case of the North Carolina License. Enjoy!

item.description:
Anna is a legendary ultrarunner and two-time winner of the Hardrock 100. Thomas had a great conversation with her about running and life.


Follow Anna on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/annafrosty

item.title: 287 | Puma MagMax Nitro, Carny Clowns & Camping, There Will Be Blood
item.description:
Things don't stop around here, as we're in the throes of fall marathon training. Meg goes full emo and bleeds herself dry for the long run (thanks to some hardcore chafing), Robbe heads for the hills of Pennsylvania to take down some carny clowns, and Thomas goes full voodoo doll with his dry needling regimen. And then there's the Puma MagMax, a shoe that belongs in Mad Max (and we're okay with that).

item.title: 286 | Cameron Balser, Running the Perimeter of the United States
item.description:
We've interviewed transcontinental runners like the legendary Mike Wardian, but this is a first. Cameron Balser is an ultrarunner and Mount to Coast athlete who recently finished a nearly year-long journey running the perimeter of the United States, averaging nearly 40 miles a day over that time, entirely self-supported. We talk about the challenges he faced, the people he met along the way, and his pair of Mount to Coast R1 that he wore for his last leg, racking up 1,100 miles with plenty of life still left.
Follow Cameron on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/camerondeanbalser/
Check out Mount to Coast: https://mounttocoast.com/

item.title: 285 | Paris Recap, Favorite Olympic Moments, Berlin & Chicago Marathons are Comin'
item.description:
Dreams do come true, folks. The team headed to Paris last week, so we decided to check out some of the local events, like the Olympic Games. There was dancing and cheering and food and drinks. Group runs galore, where Robbe wore his Legionnaire to the Louvre, a modern art masterpiece if there ever was one. It was truly an amazing experience and we're glad we got to see a bunch of you in the process. Not, it's time to get ready for Berlin and Chicago. We'll sleep when we're dead.
Looking to run sub-4 at Chicago? Join our unofficial group to help pace you along the way. DM Robbe at @normcorerunner on Instagram (no feet pics plz) or email info@bigrunmedia.com

item.title: 284 | Deena & Andrew Kastor, Olympic Bronze Medalist & Coach
item.description: This is a super special episode of The Drop, as Thomas and Meaghan interview the dynamic duo of Deena and Andrew Kastor. Deena won bronze in the marathon distance at the 2004 Olympic Games and held the American women's record in the distance from 2006 to 2022. Andrew is the head coach of the Mammoth Track Club and also coaches Meg and Thomas! We talk about everything from training to nutrition to marriage and have a lot of fun in the process.

item.title: 282 | Drew Petersen, Ultrarunner, Pro Skier & Mental Health Advocate
item.description:
Professional skier Drew Petersen had everything he could want in life, but inside, he was falling apart. He finally found help and turned his life around and has committed himself to helping others do the same. This was a really great talk about mental health, the struggles that many of us deal with, and the light that comes when we realize we're not alone and asking for help is not a weakness. Along the way, Drew found ultrarunning and he will be running his hometown race– the Leadville 100– this August, while releasing his documentary Feel it All at the sound of the starting gun.
To watch the trailer for Feel It All, purchase a hat, or learn more about the Feel It All events tour, please visit https://drew-petersen.com/feelitall

item.title: 281 | New Balance SC Trainer v3, Hospital Rooms, Summer Running & More
item.description:
Summer GRIT has officially come to a close, so we highlight some of the top performances from this year's participants. We also talk about our own summer running, which we're kind of over with at this point. Also, a hotel that recreates sound machine environments should probably be a thing if someone wants to do that. We also talk about the New Balance SC Trainer v3, a plated super trainer that is both similar and different to the versions before.

item.title: 278 | Joe Rubio, Running Warehouse Co-Founder
item.description:
A walking encyclopedia of run knowledge, Joe Rubio is the co-founder of Running Warehouse, our go-to place for all things running shoes and gear. We were out in California recently and stopped by their warehouse for a rare, in-studio interview. We cover a wide range of topics, from youth run culture to running shoe history to hot takes on various running shoe brands. Such a good time, hope you enjoy. Shop at Running Warehouse for all your shoe needs ➡ https://bit.ly/RUNNING-WAREHOUSE


Listen to "Mr. Rubio Used to Run" podcast ➡ https://open.spotify.com/show/2WqAgQT7rr4MkhL25EA4fE?si=0a6162c625484be8

item.description:

item.title: 270 | Nick Bare on Going All-In and Overcoming Adversity
item.description:
One of our most requested guests is finally here. Meet the man who kickstarted the hybrid athlete movement and also ran a blistering 2:39 at CIM this past December– Nick Bare. We talk about growing up in Pennsylvania, race day excuses, going all-in on your vision, his roots and journey as a hybrid athlete, parenthood, and more.

Follow Nick on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@nickbarefitness

Follow Nick on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nickbarefitness

Check out Bare Performance Nutrition: https://www.bareperformancenutrition.com/

We also talk about our experiences in the Puma Deviate Nitro Elite 3, a carbon-plated race day shoe found on the feet of some of the top runners at the Olympic Marathon Trials. SUPPORT OUR SPONSORS!

item.title: 267 | Adidas Adizero SL 2, Robbe's Run Retreat, Fireworks & Stuff
item.description:
There's been a lot of running going on around here, from Global Running Day to run retreats to prepping for Broken Arrow with Salomon (where you all should hang out with us). We talk everything from CBN to fireworks to sprint car racing, so there's something weird for everyone. We also cover the surprisingly great Adidas Adizero SL 2 and a super secret and special Hoka trail shoe that we'll be wearing for our Western States watching. We're at Broken Arrow with Salomon in ONE WEEK! Don't miss out on the action, here are the details, these events are FREE for everyone: Thursday, June 20 4pm-6pm | Salomon Trail Shoe Demo & Shakeout Run Salomon/KT Sundeck Vendor Village Friday, June 21 5pm-6pm | Salomon Trail Shoe Demo & Shakeout Run + FREE BUCKET HAT GIVEAWAY Salomon/KT Sundeck Vendor Village Sunday, June 23 7am-8:30am | Salomon Trail Shoe Demo & Believe in the Run Golden Trail Series Cheer Squad with @tfneuberger & @normcorerunner SUPPORT OUR SPONSORS!

item.title: 266 | Becs Gentry, Peloton Instructor, 2:32 Marathoner
item.description: Becs Gentry is a peloton instructor, 2:32 marathoner, a mom, and an all-around amazing human. We first spoke with Becs on her podcast, Set the Pace, and immediately knew we wanted to hear more. Originally from London, Becs now lives in New York City teaching tread, strength and rowing classes. In this episode, we chat all about growing up in the UK, her time with Nike, running in the Olympic Marathon Trials and how she ended up at Peloton.

item.title: 264 | Matt Niutta, Fractel Founder
item.description:
Keeping cool is key in the summer months and your running hat has a lot to do with it. Robbe talks to Matt Niutta, founder of Fractel, an Australian-based headwear brand looking to make the best products for runners. One of those products is the Fractel x BITR Robbe Raccoon Signature Legionnaire (available now!). This is more of a fireside chat than a business talk, mostly because Robbe is primarily concerned with the state of Australian wildlife, so stick around for that sort of stuff.
Snag the Robbe Raccoon Signature Legionnaire at 6 p.m. ET on Monday, June 3: https://shop.believeintherun.com/
Shop Fractel: https://fractel.us/

Finally, we chat about the new On Cloudrunner 2, which may or may not be your best stability option.

item.title: 262 | Bobby Imamura, Teacher and ALS Running Partner
item.description:
After a late start to running, Los Angeles math teacher Bobby Imamura became hooked on marathons, just like the rest of us. But at one point, he wasn't sure why. Until he started running with ALS (formerly known as Lou Gherig's disease) athletes, and seeing their goals become his own. Along the way, with the support of Tata Consultancy Services, they nearly broke the world record for running a marathon with a wheelchair while making an impact on all those around him. In April, Bobby finished the London Marathon for his sixth and final star of the Abbott World Marathon Majors.
Watch Bobby's documentary on YouTube: https://youtu.be/JwaFDStDb0I

item.title: 257 | Mizuno Neo Vista, Berlin Marathon Entry Giveaway, Custom Netflix Algorithms
item.description:
We're back in the regular swing of things, which means more random talk about our everyday lives, which actually includes running. Speaking of running, we're giving away two free guaranteed entries into the 2024 Berlin Marathon, thanks to our friends at Near Earth! They make the best running socks on earth, and now they're giving you an entry to one of the best races on the planet as well. We also cover the all-new Mizuno Neo Vista, which may end up being one of our favorite shoes so far this year.

Learn more and enter here (open to U.S. residents only): https://mailchi.mp/believeintherun/berlinmarathonentry

item.title: 256 | Christian Brecheis, Founder of Near Earth
item.description:
Photographer first, small business owner second, Christian Brecheis founded Near Earth socks in the middle of the pandemic. Engineered in Germany and made in Europe with premium knits using 100% renewable energy, the socks are designed for the optimal running performance. We get an inside look at what it takes to do that and hear about his business and running journey along the way. Check out Near Earth: https://nearearth.run/en-us

Follow Christian on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/christianbrecheis/

Want a free bib to the 2024 Berlin Marathon? Enter here: https://mailchi.mp/believeintherun/berlinmarathonentry

item.title: 255 | European Road Trip Recap
item.description:
We're finally back home in the good ol' U.S. of A, but our journey to Europe was worth it. The team recounts their days in Italy at the Diadora event before giving a full recap of a few days spent at Adidas HQ in Germany for the fourth annual "Road to Records" event. It was a long and winding road, but the future of running is bright and we were glad to witness it firsthand.

item.title: 254 | Emily Halnon, Author and Trail Runner
item.description: Completing a circuit of all the Domino's Pizza in Eugene during one run may be her coolest feat, but Emily Halnon also holds the overall FKT on the 460-mile Oregon section of the Pacific Crest Trail (PCT) which is certainly the most impressive. Her upcoming book, "To The Gorge," explores that week-long journey and her reflections on the death of her mother after a battle with uterine cancer. Learn more about Emily and pre-order her book here: https://www.emilyhalnon.com/

item.title: 253 | The Gang Visits Venice
item.description: We made it to Italy but our luggage didn't, so we were missing some podcast mics for this episode. Nevertheless, we worked with what we had. Live from a hotel room in Venice, the gang recaps their journey and first day in the city, as they headed over to check out the newest technology and product from Diadora as they unveiled it all at Venice’s famed Arsenale, with an emphasis on Italian craftsmanship and the cultural influence of Venice on sports and footwear. We also got a glimpse into our latest Olympic innovations and a chance to talk to leadership about the future of the brand. SUPPORT OUR SPONSORS! item.title: 252 | Dr. Annie Neurohr, Physical Therapist
item.description:
Our favorite PT is back for round two! In this episode, we cover a bunch of the PT questions you had last time but we didn't get to answer, as well as some new ones, including Achilles injuries, plantar fasciitis, strengthening the pelvic floor, and more. Dr. Annie Neurohr is a lifelong runner and physical therapist at Sinai Running Rehabilitation Program here in Baltimore.
Follow Dr. Neurohr on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/pace_doctor/

item.title: 251 | The Boston Marathon Recap
item.description:
We almost split this into two podcasts, but instead, you get the whole rundown in one shot (for better or for worse). We went up to Boston, we had an absolute blast with our New Balance group run and meet and greet on Friday, and Saturday our mind-blowing Asics run and cap giveaway on Sunday, and of course– the crown jewel of running, the 128th Boston Marathon, thanks to Adidas. There were moments of pure joy and others of slight regret. It all came together for an unforgettable weekend that we'd do a hundred times over if we could. Thank you to everyone showed up and said hello during the race, and who made this weekend a true success. You are the reason we do this and can keep doing this, and you're more of an inspiration than you know.

item.title: 250 | Jean Pourrat, Sports Manager for Veja
item.description:
Few brands truly embrace the idea of sustainability, but Veja practices what they preach from beginning to end. Jean Pourrat is the sports manager for the French brand and worked on the development of the all-new Condor 3. We go into all the details of Veja, their mission, and how they made a shoe that seamlessly blends performance and style.

Check out Veja Running: https://www.veja-store.com/en_us/running-shoes


Follow Jean Pourrat on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jean_pourrat/

item.title: 248 | Lindsay Flanagan, ASICS Athlete
item.description: One of our first podcast guests ever is back on The Drop after almost four years away. Lindsay is an Asics pro athlete and elite women's marathoner who recently finished 8th at the U.S. Olympic Marathon Trials. We get an inside look at her training, running high mileage, and what it's like living in Australia. Side note: she's also very good at trivia.

item.title: 247 | On Cloudmonster Hyper, Easter Eggin', Panic Attacks, Cider Talk
item.description:
It's taper time for Boston and the gang is barely holding on. Illness, dislocated shoulders, and panic attacks- we can barely fit it all in. Special guest Jarrett joins us this week to talk about his apple cider basicness and getting shamed by Patient First doctors, Thomas needs to the hype of Chipotle to come back, and Meg tries her best to keep Dune out of the conversation. We also have special guest check-ins thanks to our friend Lin who stopped by HQ. Last but not least, we talk about the On Cloudmonster Hyper.

item.dc:creator: Believe in the Run
item.itunes:summary:
It's taper time for Boston and the gang is barely holding on. Illness, dislocated shoulders, and panic attacks- we can barely fit it all in. Special guest Jarrett joins us this week to talk about his apple cider basicness and getting shamed by Patient First doctors, Thomas needs to the hype of Chipotle to come back, and Meg tries her best to keep Dune out of the conversation. We also have special guest check-ins thanks to our friend Lin who stopped by HQ. Last but not least, we talk about the On Cloudmonster Hyper.

item.description:

item.title: 244 | Gabi Rooker, Nike Pro
item.description:
Since her marathon debut at Grandma’s Marathon in 2021 to her most recent race at the Chicago Marathon, Rooker has improved upon her personal best from 2:54 to 2:24. We talk about growing up as a gymnast and how she made the transition from short distance to marathon racing.
Follow Gabi on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/gabirooker/

item.title: 243 | TYR Valkyrie Elite Carbon, Popped Shoulders, Meg's West Coast Trip
item.description:
It's looking like an infirmary at the BITR offices, with popped shoulders and illness and stress fractures. We'll be okay. Tune in to hear about Meg's journey to the West Coast to celebrate the 40th anniversary of Joan Benoit-Samuelson's 1984 Olympic gold with Nike. Then stick around for a recount of Thomas and Robbe's long run adventures from this past weekend. We also cover two brand-new shoes: the TYR Valkyrie Elite Carbon and Mount to Coast R1. Susan's Cookies: https://www.instagram.com/sjmeier2/ Our Favorite Water Bottles Hydrapak Skyflask 500ml: https://bit.ly/3P56n4k Nathan Speeddraw: https://bit.ly/493T7nG

item.title: 242 | Rob Perez, Run Content Creator
item.description:
From living on a submarine in the Navy to running ultras across the Atacama in Chile, Rob Perez has been to all ends of the extremes. We talk about his journey as a runner, run culture in D.C. and beyond, and what it's like as a content creator in 2024.
Follow Rob on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/r.oh.bee/

item.title: 241 | Annapolis Half Marathon Recap, Florida Adventures, Sugar Gliders
item.description:
Thomas and Meaghan test the legs out at the Annapolis Half Marathon, Robbe finds a money trap in Florida, and we need all of you to write you local congressperson and get the laws on raccoon ownership changed. We also discuss the newly unveiled 2024 Boston Marathon medal.

Learn more about the Golden Trail Series powered by Salomon: https://www.goldentrailseries.com/ This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. item.title: 240 | Eric Floberg, Filmmaker, YouTuber, Run Content Creator
item.description:
Another legend from Chicago, Eric Floberg is a videographer, photographer, YouTuber, and Instagram creator producing some of the highest quality run content out there. He's also on a journey to drop his marathon time from 4:00 to 2:35. We had a great conversation about all things running and life.
Follow Eric on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@flobergruns
Follow Eric on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/floberg.runs/

item.title: 238 | Kevin Salvano, Olympic Trials Marathoner
item.description: If you listen to the podcast, you already know. We were going hard in the paint for the Kevin Salvano during the Olympic Marathon Trials and while it may not have been his day, he went full send and that's all that matters. Find out how he got to the Trials in the first place and what it's like to "quit running" and then run a 2:16 for your first marathon.

item.title: 237 | Asics Metaspeed Sky/Edge Paris, Charleston, Godzilla, Beyoncé and Crepes
item.description: Meg and Thomas return from Charleston and Thomas comes back with an accent that linguistically lands somewhere between Colonel Sanders and eastern European. We also get into some edge-of-the-seat commentary on produce storage, Godzilla, and running updates as we train for Boston. We also give our thoughts on the Asics Metaspeed Sky Paris and Edge Paris, which are the best race day shoes Asics has ever put out. item.title: 236 | Ben True, Northwood Athletics Coach
item.description:
Repping that northern attitude out in the wilds of New Hampshire, Ben True is a decorated runner across various distances who took 7th place in the 2021 New York City Marathon in his first race at that distance. He currently runs Northwood Athletics, a grassroots effor to grow the next national running hub for all distances, all surfaces, and all abilities. We had a great conversation about his career, training solo, and why the East Coast has our hearts on lock.
Learn more about Northwoods: https://www.northwoodsathletics.com/
Follow Ben on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/benjtrue/

item.title: 235 | Hoka Mach 6, Fear & Claustrophobing, The Bear & The Bachelor
item.description: We go where other podcasts don't dare to go. Mainly because other podcasters aren't finding copius amounts of watter bottles filled with urine on their runs and talking about it. We also talk about our running, our fears, our favorite shows right now, and the Hoka Mach 6 - a lightweight, fun, and more durable trainer that Hoka fans will/should/probably love. Learn more about the Golden Trail Series powered by Salomon: https://www.goldentrailseries.com/ This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. We use it to rehydrate after all our hard efforts and it is hands down the best hydration and recovery mix out there. We're super excited about the new LMNT Chocolate Medley, featuring Chocolate Mint, Chocolate Chai, and Chocolate Raspberry, designed to be enjoyed hot or swirled into your favorite recipes.

item.title: 232 | Steff Gardner, Salomon Marketing Manager
item.description:
If you want to hear about animal attacks, skydiving, and basejumping, boy, do we have a podcast for you. Steff Gardner is the marketing manager for Salomon North America, but she's also a seasoned trail runner, skydiver, basejumper, and a whole bunch of other things. This, despite growing up in the great indoors. We have a really fun conversation about all those things, and also about Salomon trail running and the "all are welcome" Golden Trail Series (which we'll be at in June!).
Learn more about the Golden Trail Series: https://www.goldentrailseries.com/
Follow Steff on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/crashboombang/

item.title: 231 | Olympic Marathon Trials Recap
item.description:
Well, it was magic down in Orlando. Over the course of three days, we packed it in. From shake out runs to podcasts to random encounters with many of you on the streets, we had an absolute blast. Of course, it was all capped off with a race to remember on Saturday morning, where we finally got to see who will be representing the United States in Paris this summer. Thanks to Hoka and Citius Mag for partnering with us throughout the week, Justin Hall (@justin_yall) and Chase Fleming (@chaseflemingphoto) for capturing photos, and thanks to all of you for coming out and supporting us and the running community. Now let's to go France. 


Watch the YouTube video recap: https://youtu.be/CGyoCHEfRko
Read the Hoka Cielo X1 review: https://believeintherun.com/shoe-reviews/hoka-cielo-x1-review/


This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. We use it to rehydrate after all our hard efforts and it is hands down the best hydration and recovery mix out there. We're super excited about the new LMNT Chocolate Medley, featuring Chocolate Mint, Chocolate Chai, and Chocolate Raspberry, designed to be enjoyed hot or swirled into your favorite recipes.
Our listeners can get a free LMNT sample pack with the purchase of any order. Pick it up here ➡ http://drinklmnt.com/thedrop

item.title: 230 | A Conversation with Hoka at the Olympic Marathon Trials
item.description:
We're working on a Hoka ska band and are stuck between SKAZ Elite, The Mighty Mighty Cliftones, Reel Big Bondis, or Hoka Mach Sax. You'll get it after you watch the interview. Live from the Hoka Fly Lab at the Olympic Marathon Trials in Orlando, Robbe and Meaghan chat with the Hoka team about how they got here and where things are going, specifically with the Hoka Cielo X1. First up, we talk with Bekah Broe, Director of Product, Performance Footwear, before jumping into all things Cielo X1 with the designer of the shoe, Val Weilert, Senior Design Manager for Hoka.


Read our review of the Hoka Cielo X1: https://believeintherun.com/shoe-reviews/hoka-cielo-x1-review/

item.title: 229 | Hoka Cielo X1, Olympic Trials, Kevin Salvano, Grit Party Recap
item.description: It's Olympic Trials week for the marathon and we're heading to Orlando (we're actually already there). But don't worry, we still found time to get weird for y'all. The big news this week is the release of the Hoka Cielo X1, a carbon-plated super shoe with a full Peba midsole and aggressive rocker geometry. It may be the most fun shoe we've ever worn. Before that, we talk about our favorite dark horse to win the Trials, our weekend at the Winter GRIT party, skinless dogs, and staring at ourselves in the mirrors to try and discover what we're made of. If that sounds bizarre to you, you're not wrong. This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. We use it to rehydrate after all our hard efforts and it is hands down the best hydration and recovery mix out there. We're super excited about the new LMNT Chocolate Medley, featuring Chocolate Mint, Chocolate Chai, and Chocolate Raspberry, designed to be enjoyed hot or swirled into your favorite recipes. Our listeners can get a free LMNT sample pack with the purchase of any order. Pick it up here ➡ http://drinklmnt.com/thedrop

item.title: 228 | Aubrey Mvula, Content Creator
item.description:
We love our content creator friends from across the pond, and Aubrey Mvula (@aubreyrunning on YouTube) is no exception. We talk about how he got started in running and into shoe reviews, how he just ran a new 10K PR, some of his favorite shoes to run in right now, and the balance of maintaining relationships with brands while still giving honest reviews. 
Check out Aubrey's channel: https://www.youtube.com/@AubreyRunning

item.title: 227 | Diadora Gara Carbon, Taylor's Comin' to Town, Sleep Masks
item.description:
Meg may or may not have set up a campsite in Harbor East in the hopes of catching a Taylor Swift sighting as she comes into town to see her Travvy lose to Lamar and the rest of the Ravens. We also cover our own running (the road to Boston begins now, or something), Thomas listening to the sounds of the Rainforest Cafe through a sleep mask, and whether or not we could survive an Andes plane crash (we couldn't). We also talk about the Diadora Gara Carbon, a surprisingly great carbon-plated race day shoe coming at the end of February.


This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. We use it to rehydrate after all our hard efforts and it is hands down the best hydration and recovery mix out there. We're super excited about the new LMNT Chocolate Medley, featuring Chocolate Mint, Chocolate Chai, and Chocolate Raspberry, designed to be enjoyed hot or swirled into your favorite recipes.


Our listeners can get a free LMNT sample pack with the purchase of any order. Pick it up here ➡ http://drinklmnt.com/thedrop


We are getting PUMPED for Marathon Trials Weekend and if you’re going to be in Orlando, we want to hang! Here’s where you can find us:


THURSDAY, FEB 1: Join us at 6:30 PM at the Orlando Track Shack and be the first group of runners in the world to demo HOKA's latest racing product. Fun miles and good times guaranteed. We'll also be giving a way a bunch of Goodr sunglasses.


Register here: https://events.hoka.com/shakeattheshackwithhokaxtracks


FRIDAY, FEB 2: We’re joining Citius Mag crew for a jog at 5 PM from the CITIUS Cafe (presented by HOKA) followed by a final marathon prediction show and some hangs. Get there at 4:30 if you want to demo the new prototype shoe. Get a beer on the house and some Jeni's ice cream for dessert.


Register here: https://events.hoka.com/hokaxcitiuscafeshakeoutrun


SATURDAY, FEB 3: We’ll be cheering our faces off at the HOKA FLY ZONE as we get to watch America's most exciting marathon of the year. (Location TBD, somewhere near the start/finish).
All events are FREE!


INDEX

00:00 LMNT

01:30 Intro

06:40 Olympic Trials

13:52 Taylor in Baltimore

16:07 Winter GRIT

33:15 Poll Questions

38:40 Boston Marathon

50:15 Thomas Instagram Buy

57:11 What's in the box?

1:03:21 Society of the Snow

1:10:12 Diadora Gara Carbon

1:18:27 Nick Bare

item.title: 226 | Dr. Annie Neurohr, Physical Therapist
item.description:
Well, this is probably part one of many to come because we only scratched the surface of all your listener questions (thanks for sending them in). Dr. Annie Neurohr is a lifelong runner and physical therapist at Sinai Running Rehabilitation Program here in Baltimore. She's singlehandedly responsible for saving Robbe's running career after a years-long bout of hamstring tendinopathy and has helped scores of other injured runners get back on track again. We talk about a wide range of topics in this episode, from pre- and post-workout routines to IT Band Syndrome to shin splints and so much more. 
Follow Dr. Neurohr on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/pace_doctor/

item.title: 225 | Adidas Takumi Sen 10, Houston Marathon, Rainforest Cafe, Jaw Workouts
item.description:
In what may be the first official "Thomas Instagram Buys" segment, we cover some key jaw workouts while Meg tries not to laugh for five minutes straight. We also talk about doing a six-star finishing medal for visiting all remaining locations of the Rainforest Cafe, and believe it or not, recap the results of the Houston Marathon. Eventually, we talk about the Adidas Takumi Sen 10. Get all the details and RSVP for the Winter GRIT party: https://mailchi.mp/believeintherun/wintergritparty2024 Pick up the Carhartt Waterproof Mittens that Robbe was talking about: https://amzn.to/3vAMsU2 This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. We use it to rehydrate after all our hard efforts and it is hands down the best hydration and recovery mix out there. We're super excited about the new LMNT Chocolate Medley, featuring Chocolate Mint, Chocolate Chai, and Chocolate Raspberry, designed to be enjoyed hot or swirled into your favorite recipes. Our listeners can get a free LMNT sample pack with the purchase of any order. Pick it up here ➡ http://drinklmnt.com/thedrop

item.title: 224 | Nathan Riech, Asics Athlete & Paralympic World Champion
item.description: After suffering a traumatic brain injury at the age of 10, Nathan Riech suffered coordination impairments on the right side of his body. Limiting his performance against able-bodied athletes, the injury fell under the guidelines for the T38 division of the Paralympics, in which Riech has become a 2x world champion.

item.description:
This episode is packed down and out with a wide array of information ranging from invaluable to "udderly" useless. Robbe recaps his 50K in the Brooks Caldera 7, Meg is CIM famous, and Thomas is singlehandedly keeping Instagram ads alive. We also find out what a MAMIL is, before diving into the New Balance Rebel v4.

item.title: 222 | Alex Hermanson, Run Influencer (@herm.runs)
item.description:
If you don't know Alex, you should. As a big guy runner, he's normalizing running for everyone and doing it in an authentic way through his TikTok videos and Instagram reels. We brought him into the studio to talk about... honestly– everything under the sun. This is the longest guest episode we've ever recorded and it probably could've kept going if we all didn't need a bathroom break. 
Follow Alex on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/herm.runs/
Follow Alex on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@hermruns

item.title: 221 | 2023 Year in Review, Asics Gel-Nimbus 26, "Intimate" Robots, Botulism
item.description: What a year it's been, and what a year it will be. On this episode of The Drop, we recap 2023 before talking about more important stuff like ventriloquism, sex robots, botulism, and runner road rage. Also, we talk about the Asice Gel-Nimbus 26, another solid daily trainer for 2024. This is episode is sponsored by LMNT. item.title: 220 | Brian Metzler, Executive Editor of Outside Run
item.description:
A veteran voice in running journalism, Brian Metzler is an expert on all things running, especially shoes and gear. As a longtime writer for the top publications in the industry and current executive editor of Outside Run, he's seen trends come and go and has personally run through them all. In this episode, we talk about all of that and more, while trying to predict what the future holds for run footwear.
Follow Brian on Instagram:

https://www.instagram.com/brian___metzler/

item.title: 219 | Reebok Floatzig, Dirty Crab Cakes, Wrecked iPhones
item.description: We're in the Bermuda Triangle of our run seasons. Meg's disoriented with no race goals on the horizon. Gravity may be on strike. Bodies and iPhones are breaking down left and right. Somewhere in there, Christmas happened. And we also ran in the Reebok Floatzig and were surprised at how much we enjoyed it.

item.title: 218 | Sarah Clancy, Founder of Sarah Marie Running Co.
item.description:
Sure, talking about running to anyone who will listen is great, but it's even better to show it off with your apparel. Sarah Clancy is the founder of Sarah Marie Running Co. and Sarah Marie Design Studio, specializing in running goods and apparel with specialty designs and slogans. Borne two years ago, her small business has taken off, but hasn't been without its rollercoaster ride of struggles and victories. This is a great episode for all those entrepreneurs out there, hope you enjoy it.


Follow Sarah Marie Running Co. on Instagram
Check out Sara Marie's website: https://sarahmarie.run/

item.title: 217 | Puma Fast-R Nitro Elite 2, Catfishing, Gift Giving, Early Aughts Shoes
item.description:
It's the most wonderful time of the year to talk about gift giving, Thomas childhood trauma and Robbe almost getting catfished with fish. There's also some skullduggery in the office fantasy football competition. Plus, we reminisce about our first running shoes, early aughts television, and run down a few different shoes, including the Puma Fast-R Nitro Elite 2, Reebok Floatzig, and Asics GEL-NYC. Merry Christmas to all!

Lagoon Pillows is how we get our good sleep and we're happy to have them sponsor the podcast. Right now you can save 15% off your first order with code BELIEVE. Shop here: https://lagoonsleep.com/believe

item.title: 216 | Erin Bailey, Influencer Management
item.description:
Starting a company managing influencers and runners while trying to be one yourself is quite the juggling act. On this episode, we talk about how Erin juggles both of those things, how deals and contracts work, and how she started running, including her involvement with Green Point Runners, supported by Brooklyn-based Bandit Running.

Follow Erin on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/erin.k.bailey
Check out Momentum Management: https://momentummanagement.co/

item.title: 215 | Meg's CIM Recap & Nike Alphafly 3
item.description:
If if it feels like we're doing a Meg race recap every other month, it's because we are. Her sixth marathon of 2023 was a final Hail Mary at an OTQ, and though it came up short, she finished with a sub-2:37 in our hearts. Get her full recap of the race, which she ran in the Nike Alphafly 3, a shoe that returns the magic that v2 took away.
Lagoon Pillows is how we get our good sleep and we're happy to have them sponsor the podcast. Right now you can save 15% off your first order with code BELIEVE. Shop here: https://lagoonsleep.com/believe

item.title: 214 | Daniel Fang, Turnstile
item.description:
Thank you for letting us be ourselves. Few people have repped Baltimore better than Daniel Fang, drummer for hardcore band Turnstile (and many others). Even with their massive rise over the past year, including a summer tour with Blink-182 and three Grammy nominations (losing to Brandi Carlile for best rock song is an absolute travesty), he remains the chillest and nicest guy you could hope to meet. It was an honor to have him into the studio to talk about art, inspiration, living a straight edge lifestyle while on tour, and his newfound love of running. Show a bit of TLC.


Listen to Turnstile on Spotify
Follow Turnstile on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/turnstileluvconnection/
Follow Daniel on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/danielfang/

item.description:
Fresh off an absolutely bonkers week at The Running Event 2023, Thomas and Robbe recap what's hot and what's not for 2024 in the running shoe world. Meaghan is in California where she ran a certain race in Sacramento in a certain shoe that starts with Alpha ends with Fly 3, which we will talk about all of that on the next episode of The Drop. There's a lot to cover in this episode, and Robbe's concerned that Thomas has never dipped french fries in a Wendy's Frosty.

item.title: 212 | Frank Sanchez, Physician Associate and Runner
item.description: Frank Sanchez is Chief Physician Associate-Cardiothoracic Surgery at University of Miami Transplant for Heart and Lung adult and pediatrics. With 25 years of experience, he has had the pleasure of taking care of the Miami football team at one point and currently is the Medical Director for LifeTime Fitness running and endurance events including Miami Marathon and Half. He is also a 7x Ironman finisher and 16x marathon finisher as well as a USA Triathlon coach and XC coach. We answer listener questions that hopefully help with your training and race day performance.

item.itunes:summary: Frank Sanchez is Chief Physician Associate-Cardiothoracic Surgery at University of Miami Transplant for Heart and Lung adult and pediatrics. With 25 years of experience, he has had the pleasure of taking care of the Miami football team at one point and currently is the Medical Director for LifeTime Fitness running and endurance events including Miami Marathon and Half. He is also a 7x Ironman finisher and 16x marathon finisher as well as a USA Triathlon coach and XC coach. We answer listener questions that hopefully help with your training and race day performance.

item.title: 211 | Karissa Schweizer, Nike Athlete
item.description: Karissa Schweizer is an American middle- and long-distance runner, Olympian and World Record holder in the women's 4x1500 meters relay. She competed in the 2020 Tokyo Olympics in the 5000 meters and 10,000 meters events finishing 11th and 12th, respectively. In 2020, she set the indoor 3000 meters American Record with a time of 8:25.70.

item.title: 210 | Thanksgiving Turkey Trots, Flute Fun and Dreamcatchers, Running in the Cold
item.description:
The team gears up for some Turkey Trots and The Running Event, discusses the new Andre 3000 album, and gives you their thoughts about running in the cold. We also discuss some of the mishaps at the Philadelphia Half Marathon. No new shoe this week, but we'll be back with tons of shoe craziness from the Running Event next week.

item.title: 209 | Tommie Runz, PR Project
item.description:
If you know us, you know him. Tommie Runz is a runner and founder of the PR Project and The Run Eat Sleep Show. His running journey began in 2018 after choosing a life of sobriety in 2017, in addition to practicing veganism. He's also a frequent co-host of the Relay podcast, where he discusses running and life with other members of the collective.


Follow Tommie on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/tommie_runz
Learn more about him: https://www.tommierunz.com/

item.title: 208 | On Cloudeclipse, Tommy Bahama, Swifties Gone Wild
item.description:
Some of us went on a bougie vacation to the islands last week, others ran 90 miles (you can probably guess which ones). Thomas makes us jealous with his tales of the Caribbean life, Robbe gets out his pent-up frustrations on the ubiquity of Taylor Swift in all aspects of life, and Meg just wants to listen to country music in peace. We also talk about the On Cloudeclipse, a shoe in which Meg has already logged 100 miles.

item.title: 207 | Michael Ko (aka Kofuzi), YouTube Creator
item.description: No introduction needed, Kofuzi is a now-legend in the shoe reviewer space and our favorite YouTuber out there. Outside of shoe reviews, he's also one of our favorite people to hang out with because he's 100% always down for a good time. Which is what we do on this episode of The Drop. item.title: 206 | New York City Marathon Recap & New Balance SC Elite v4
item.description:
Is the New York City Marathon weekend a national holiday? At this point, it should be. Our last stop on the train to NYC took us right into the heart of marathon weekend, where we met and hung out with so many of you. From the expo to the shake out run with New Balance to the marathon itself, we're not sure anything can top what just went down. Hear all about our full in-depth recap of the weekend and the race itself, and forgive us if we get a little lost along the way.


Special thanks to New Balance and New York Road Runners, and of course, to all of you. We really have a special community of runners and couldn't do it it without your support.


Lagoon Pillows is how we get our good sleep. Right now you can save $50 on a Performance Pack or $75 on the Peak Sleep Set. Shop here: https://lagoonsleep.com/believe

item.title: 205 | Dom Scott, Adidas Athlete
item.description:
From South Africa to Arkansas to Colorado, Dom Scott has literally chased her running dream the whole way around the world. From going to boarding school as a teen to training with Emma Bates on Team Boss to finishing with a 2:27 at last month's Chicago Marathon, she's had quite the journey. We talk about all of that and more in this conversation.


Follow Dom on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/domscottrunsa/

item.title: 204 | Asics Novablast 4, Robbe's Big Secret, NYC Here We Come
item.description: Carmen Sandiego taught us nothing about geography and it shows (sorry, Europe). But it's the calm before the storm as we embrace the empire state of mind in our lead-up to this weekend's New York City Marathon. We also go deeper personally than we ever have, thanks to Robbe's mid-podcast confessional. Of course, we also talk about the Asics Novablast 4, one of the most-anticipated running shoes of 2023.

item.title: 203 | Cameron Hanes, Speedland Athlete & Bowhunter
item.description:
From hunting in the backcountry to running ultras, Cam Hanes knows the importance of endurance when it comes to challenging endeavors. He's the author of several books, frequent guest on the Joe Rogan Experience, host of the Keep Hammering Collective podcast, and now– a Speedland athlete In this espisode, we talk about the mindset it takes to dig deep (and our mutual admiration for Sally McRae and Courtney Dauwalter), whether greatness is innate or learned, and his all-new Speedland trail shoe model– the GS:PGH.


Check out the all-new GS:PGH from Speedland: https://www.runspeedland.com/


Follow Cam Hanes on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/cameronrhanes/


Check out Cam's website & Keep Hammering Collective podcast: https://cameronhanes.com/

item.title: 201 | Emily Oster, Economist and Author
item.description: As runners, we obviously love data. But what about as parents? Emily Oster is a parent, professor, New York Times bestselling author, runner, and data-driven economist focused on helping other parents navigate life. And she's a fan of The Drop! We had a lot of fun talking to her about her approach to data-driven parenting, running, and life. Follow Emily on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/profemilyoster/ Listen to ParentData with Emily Oster: https://pod.link/1633515294 Check out Emily's books: https://emilyoster.net/writing/

item.title: 200 | Baltimore Running Festival Recap, Rainy Day Adidas Evo 1, Bathroom Break-Ins, Girl With the Green Ribbon
item.description:
If you ever marry someone who always wears a green ribbon on their neck– DON'T ASK THEM TO REMOVE IT. Aside from that advice, we recap the Baltimore Running Festival and Thomas's experience running in the Adios Evo 1 on a rainy day and Robbe's experience in the Nike Vaporfly 3. We're also in love with e-bikes now and we don't know how to make it stop (the love, not the bike).

By the way, SAVE THE DATE: We'll be doing a New Balance x BITR NYC Marathon shakeout run on Saturday, November 4, starting and ending at LAVAN Midtown (641 W 42nd St). Starts at 10 a.m., givewaways include custom hats and sling bags to the first few hundred people.

RSVP HERE: https://mailchi.mp/believeintherun/nyc-shakeout

Easy 3-mile route with special guests Kofuzi, Matt Choi, Charlie Watson (aka @therunnerbeans), Tommie Runz & the PR Project, Carleth Keys, Alex Hermanson (@herm.runs) and more!

Get Meg's Kodiak Oatmeal Cookies Recipe: https://believeintherun.com/kodiak-peak-oatmeal-cookie-recipe-simple-snack-for-runners/

Don't forget to check out this week's sponsors!

item.title: 199 | We Asked Emma Bates to Drink Post-Chicago Marathon Martinis in Our Hotel and She Said 'Yes'
item.description: Emma Bates ran the Chicago Marathon, and Meg ran the Chicago Marathon, so that seemed like a pretty good reason to have martinis in our hotel room together while recapping the race. Obviously, it was a pretty good time. There's a lot of talk about goats, like, the actual animal. And alpacas. It's basically an advertisement for FarmersOnly.

item.title: 198 | Chicago Marathon Recap
item.description: This is it, the recap you've all been waiting for (or not). We went to Chicago, we ate some pizza, ate some ramen, drank some dranks, got up early, stayed up late, hung with friends (like you and Emma Bates), and somewhere in between, Meg ran a marathon. And she got a PR. We also held the world record-breaking Nike Alphafly 3 and give you our thoughts about that. Buckle up, it's a wild ride! Don't forget to eat your oatmeal! Kodiak Peak Oatmeal is legit awesome, we've been using it to refuel after our morning runs. Made with 100% whole grain oats and 20g of protein per packet. item.title: 197 | Erika Kemp, Brooks Athlete
item.description: Is Erika Kemp the future face of distance running in America? After posting the fastest American debut ever in the marathon at Boston earlier this year (and becoming the fastest American-born Black marathoner ever in the process), she may be on her way. We talk to the Brooks athlete on her success and the future of her running career.

item.title: 196 | Nike Vomero 17, Flame Broiled, Chicago Lead-Up, Gettin' With Ghosts
item.description:
It's spooky season around here, which means we're getting creepy with weird ghost talk. But we also manage to talk about Meg's lead-up to the Chicago Marathon and her inexperience with the value menu at Burger King. Then there's the whole discussion about inflation affecting tips for strippers, because that's the perfect lead-in to the $160 Nike Vomero 17, which is about the going price for a premium daily trainer these days.

item.title: 195 | Brian Beckstead, Altra Co-Founder
item.description: At a time when every shoe was over 10 mm in drop, Brian Beckstead and Golden Harper came along and took an idea from zero to, well... zero drop. Since then, Altra has become a staple brand in the running, walking, and hiking scenes, known for its footshape shoes and focus on a natural running lifestyle. However, everything has changed with the all-new 4 mm drop AltraFWD Experience. Does it matter? We ask Brian about that, the history and future of the brand, and what it's like to fly fish in South America. Check out Altra: https://www.altrarunning.com/ Read the review of the AltraFWD Experience ➡ https://believeintherun.com/shoe-reviews/altrafwd-experience-review/

item.title: 194 | AltraFWD Experience, Berlin Breakdown, Post-Run Freak Faces
item.description: First things first, let's talk about Kipchoge's dual snot rivers that rivaled the outflow of the Tigris and Euphrates. Then we'll talk about his win in Berlin and Assefa's dismantling of the women's world record. From there, we get into how horrible our faces look after a run, whether or not we're just perpetually injured but used to it, and wonder if Baltimore rats are getting into Meg's collagen stash. Lastly, we touch on the AltraFWD Experience, a 4 mm drop shoe that's... a shoe. If this episode doesn't help you nail your peak long run for this fall marathon cycle, then you may as well just quit running.

item.title: 193 | Emily Heller, Shoe Reviewer
item.description:
We love having friends on the podcast, especially if they're fellow shoe nerds like us. Emily is one of the few/only women shoe reviewers on YouTube, and also has the most creative channel name (@runlikeheller). Oh, and she's also on the #traintonyc team, so we're lucky we get to hang out with her a few times on our journey to running the New York City Marathon with New Balance.


We talk about how she got into running and reviewing, what life is like on Long Island, and which shoes she's loving right now.


Check out Run Like Heller on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@RunLikeHeller
Follow Emily on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/runlikeheller

item.title: 192 | Atreyu Race Model, The $500 Adidas Evo 1, Meg's Mild Injury, Canadian Booze
item.description:
Sorry to those who aren't baseball fans, but the Orioles made the playoffs, so we take some time to acknowledge the only real birds that exist in the world. Then we get into the usual randomness and try and figure out if Thomas got placebo drunk at a Guns 'n' Roses concert in 1992. Also, Meg has a minor setback three weeks out from Chicago. In shoe talk, we discuss the $500 Adidas Adizero Adios Pro Evo 1 and, on the opposite side of the spectrum, the $120 carbon-plated Atreyu Race Model.
Get our thoughts on the Adidas Evo 1: https://believeintherun.com/adidas-adizero-adios-pro-evo-1/
Read our review of the Atreyu Race Model: https://believeintherun.com/shoe-reviews/atreyu-race-model-review/

item.title: 191 | Josh Kerr, Brooks Beast Elite
item.description: He won the 5th Avenue Mile two weeks after he took down Jakob Ingebrigtsen on the sport's biggest stage, outkicking him en route to a world title in the 1500m. And now he's a guest on the greatest podcast in history of the universe. So yeah, you could say it's been a whirlwind of a month for Brooks elite Josh Kerr. We had a lot of fun talking to him about running, winning, and eating to much fast food in America. Follow Josh on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/joshhkerr

item.title: 190 | New Balance 1080v13, 5th Avenue Mile Recap, & Mike the Headless Chicken
item.description: The gang took the actual train to NYC to run the New Balance and New York Road Runners 5th Avenue Mile. We talk about the worst seat on a train, Thomas's race day regrets, and Robbe making questionable decisions about stuff found on the ground (again). We also cover the legendary life of Mike the Headless Chicken, which is marginally more entertaining than our conversation about the New Balance 1080v13, which features some major upgrades.

item.title: 188 | Salomon S/Lab Phantasm 2, Meg's 100-Mile Week, Behind The Scenes of Shoe Reviewing
item.description:
It's not an illusion: Salomon has a legit race day shoe in the S/Lab Phantasm 2. But before we talk about that, we must discuss Meg's 100-mile training week, the merits of banana splits, and the reality of reviewing shoes and how brands perceive us. The Salomon S/Lab Phantasm 2 releases on October 1 for $275.
Get all the details and RSVP for 5th Avenue Mile shakeout run in New York City on Saturday, September 9: https://mailchi.mp/believeintherun.com/5thaveshakeout

item.title: 187 | Robbie Paterson | Sr. Manager Footwear Development, Adidas Adizero Elite Racing
item.description: What's going on behind the scenes of the Adidas Adizero podium push over the last few years? That's what we ask Robbie Paterson, Senior Manager of Footwear Development in the Adizero Elite Racing line. We get all the details on how they almost had a 50 mm race shoe until World Athletics laid down the law, how much testing and development goes into an Adizero shoe, and everything that went into the Adidas Adizero Prime X 2 Strung.

item.description:

item.title: 184 | Puma Magnify Nitro 2, Falmouth Recap, Meg Does The Kofuzi Truly Challenge, Free Dunkin' Sandwiches
item.description:
We did the Cape Cod kwassa kwassa, running the Falmouth Road Race with Asics this past weekend. We recap all that, including Meg's completion of the Kofuzi Truly Challenge (and her amazing performance on race day). Robbe also scavenges someone's breakfast in the airport and you get to find out if we're gonna be cremated when we die, because why not. Oh, and we talk about the Puma Magnify Nitro 2, a max cushion trainer that we've been enjoying this week.

This episode is sponsored by Electrolit, a scientifically formulated hydration beverage that replenishes the body during physical activity, intense heat, and other outdoor warm-weather activities. It's legit, we drink it, you should check them out.

item.title: 183 | Morgan Wade, Country Music Artist
item.description:
We wish we knew her in our wilder days, but we know her now. Morgan Wade is a country music artist who also happens to love weightlifting, running, and ultrarunning. We talk about what it's like to be the Travis Barker lookalike of country music (she has a solid collection of tattoos), a woman in the industry, growing up in a small town, running trails, and more. This was a really fun conversation about running and life.

Download or stream her newest album "Psychopath," on August 25.


Listen to her debut album "Reckless," featuring her hit song "Wilder Days," wherever you get your music.


Follow Morgan on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/morganwademusic/

item.title: 181 | Greg Itahara, Visual Storyteller
item.description: Shoes and running apparel are cool, but they're a lot cooler when Greg Itahara gets his hands on them. Greg is a visual storyteller, an artist who has excelled at creating beautiful and unique designs that oftentimes find themselves on one-off shoes, including the hand-painted Asics Gel-Kayano 30 that he presented to Kayano-San himself at our office here in Baltimore. We were honored to have him in our studio to talk about art, storytelling, creativity, and more. Follow Greg on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/itahara_art/

item.title: 180 | Nike InfinityRN 4, Magician Dating Services, Megs's Get Out of Jail Free Coin
item.description: Things go sideways real quick this week as we try to test the limits of our listeners' attention spans. Thomas and Robbe discuss toothpaste squeezing methods, Thomas gets Robbe to try a drunken cookie recipe, Meg is handed a get out of jail free coin, and we also respond to the torrent of comments on our Dr. Phil Maffetone episode. One more stop on our Summer Hydration Tour! 8/19 - Boston - Summer Hydration Tour Stop #7 RSVP here: https://believeintherun.com/event/summer-hydration-tour-stop-7-boston/

item.title: 179 | Dr. Phil Maffetone, Founder of the MAF Method for Heart Rate Training
item.description:
We probably should have had him on a long time ago, but better late than never. Dr. Phil Maffetone is a coach, author, innovator, and founder of the 180 Formula and the MAF test for heart rate training. He's basically the godfather of the field, which has seen a huge surge in followers over the last decade. He's also been an advisor to music legends like Rick Rubin, Red Hot Chili Peppers, Johnny Cash, and James Taylor.


In this episode, we talk about heart rate training and its benefits, as well as his new research into music and its affect on the mind and training. This is a long one, but it was a lot of fun and super interesting!


Learn more about Dr. Maffetone's work: https://philmaffetone.com/

item.title: 177 | Brett Fraser, Enhanced Games, Chief Athletes Officer
item.description:
Look, we had to know what they were all about, so we went straight to the source. Brett Fraser is the Chief Athletes Officer for the Enhanced Games, a sporting event without drug testing, billed as "an alternative to the corrupt Olympic Games."
This is an open conversation about the persistence of drugs in sport, the idea of "scientifically-enhanced" athletes, and whether or not that's a good thing or a bad thing for sport overall. There are definitely two sides to this issue and both have certain merits, whether you agree or not. Either way, we're interested to see how it plays out over the next year and a half.
Learn more about the Enhanced Games: https://enhanced.org/

item.title: 175 | Tom Evans, Adidas Terrex, Winner of Western States 2023
item.description:
We didn't know this before we started, but apparently we just talked to the only person to podium at Marathon de Sables, UTMB, and Western States, where he just won a few weeks ago with the 4th-fastest time ever. And to think that it all started with a bet.
Tom Evans is an Adidas Terrex athlete and no doubt, one of the best ultrarunners in the world right now, throwing down incredible performances on some of the toughest courses in the game. We talk about how he got into running, why he drinks Red Bull before, during, and after his races, and how he's loving the new carbon-plated trail racing shoe coming from Adidas.
Follow Tom on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/tomevansultra/
Shop Adidas Terrex: https://bit.ly/SHOP-ADIDAS-TERREX

item.title: 174 | Nike Ultrafly, Meg Goes Full Rock Star on Tour, Lyme Margaritas
item.description:
Meg's really living up to the tour vibes as we find out when she recaps this weekend's Summer Hydration Tour visits in Austin and Miami. Robbe finds out what happens one week after you have a tick in your arm. Thomas has his own medical anomalies. And we talk about the Nike Ultrafly, which is probably one of the best Nike road shoes... but maybe not at $250.

item.title: 173 | Ben Andrews, Author and Motivational Speaker
item.description:
As a runner growing up in Portland, Ore., Ben Andrews was set to live the ultimate dream when he landed a spot on the University of Oregon track and field team. And then it all fell apart.


Andrews is a motivational speaker, realtor, and author of "The Long Run," a book about overcoming adversity and seeing your dreams through until the end. We talk about his life, his book, what running has meant to him, and how to get the most out of all of it.


Learn more about Ben and pick up The Long Run: https://www.benandrews.com/the-long-run.html

item.title: BONUS: Live with Norda at Renegade Running in Oakland
item.description:
On this special bonus episode of The Drop, Thomas and Robbe team up for a live audience podcast with Norda founders Nick and Willa Martire. Live from Renegade Running in Oakland, California. We talk about what it takes to start a shoe company (a lot), how their shoe is different from other models in the trail space, and how they navigated through certain obstacles on the way to where they are now. We also take some audience questions, which was a lot of fun.
The Norda 002 is a lightweight performance trail shoe featuring a ripstop-style Dyneema upper, Vibram midsole and Vibram Litebase with Megagrip outsole. Available now.
Shop Norda 002: https://nordarun.com/
Shop Renegade Running: https://renegade-running.com/

item.description:
Thomas and Robbe recap last weekend's trip to Oakland with Renegade Running and Norda, giving you all the behind-the-scenes commentary on 48 hours of life in the bay city. From pest control to redwood trees to sketchy bus rides up a mountain, it was an action-packed weekend. We also recap week 2 of Summer GRIT, and cover what we liked and didn't like about the Norda 002.

item.title: 171 | Alison Staples, Coach, SCI Physical Therapist Assistant, & Leader of R.I.O.T. Squad Run Club
item.description:
Alison took the unconventional route to running, having started at the age of 30. She has completed 7 full marathons, and a handful of half marathons, 10ks, and 5ks. Her love for the sport has quickly transcended into wanting to give others the same sense of accomplishment she felt after completing her first race. She is the leader of R.I.OT. Squad run club, and an SCI Physical Therapist Assistant, as well as a run coach.

Follow Alison on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/alioop_0_o_runs/

Her love for the sport has quickly transcended into wanting to give others the same sense of accomplishment she felt after completing her first race. She is the leader of R.I.OT. Squad run club, and an SCI Physical Therapist Assistant, as well as a run coach.

Follow Alison on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/alioop_0_o_runs/

item.title: 169 | Makenna Myler, Asics Pro Runner
item.description:
It's hard enough to run a 5:25 mile, but what about doing it nine months pregnant? Makenna Myler is a professional runner for Asics who qualified for the Olympic Trials in the 10k in 2021 and finished 14th less than a year after giving birth to her daughter. She's been running PRs ever since and in this episode we talk about what that looks like.


Follow Makenna on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/benzmakenz/

item.title: 168 | On Cloudboom Echo 3, Meg Hits a Sufferfest at the BAA 10K, Joe Dirt & Fireworks
item.description: Meg's in Boston at the New Balance Research Lab with the Train to NYC team, but no worries, the podcast must go on. Thomas and Robbe manage to not burn headquarters down, though next week may be a different story when we go full Joe Dirt on our stash of 4th of July fireworks. Also, we talk about the On Cloudboom Echo 3, a $290 race day shoe that may or may not live up to the price tag. Also– Summer GRIT starts on Saturday! Kind of crept up on us, so not sure we even mentioned it, but we're ready to roll. item.title: 165 | Lo Johnson, Pro Football Player & Runner
item.description: Delano Johnson is unique in the world of runners in that he's a 6'5", 250-pound Canadian football defensive end (currently a free agent). A current Baltimore-area resident, Lo played college football at Bowie State University and has played professionally for the Houston Texans, Toronto Argonauts, Portland Thunder/Steel and Hamilton Tiger-Cats. He's also a member of our Train to NYC 2 team with New Balance and we couldn't be more excited to have him along on this journey. Follow Lo on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/so_thankful10

item.title: 164 | Adidas Boston 12, Meg Figures Out Horse Betting, Shaggy & Scoobs, E-rat-ication, & More
item.description: After a tame time in Boulder, we're back in the wild, folks. That means we talk about everything from Meg's new gambling addiction (jk... maybe) to rat trapping in Baltimore to why it's absolutely criminal that Matthew Lillard wasn't nominated for an Oscar for his flawless portrayal of Shaggy in Scooby Doo 2: Monsters Unleashed. Think about it though: he really was robbed. Oh, we also talk about the Adidas Boston 12 and why it's finally come around to the greatness it once was.

item.title: 163 | Martinus Evans, Slow AF Run Club
item.description:
Passionate about showing that everyone can be a runner, Martinus Evans weighed 380 pounds when he decided to run a marathon in response to an orthopedic specialist who called him "fat." Since then, he's lost over 80 pounds, started a run club with over 10,000 members worldwide, coached others, and written a book (available now). And his journey's just getting started.

Slow AF Run Club: The Ultimate Guide for Anyone Who Wants to Run to is available now Follow Martinus on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/300poundsandrunning


This episode is sponsored by Lagoon pillows, we get crazy good sleep with them and you should too. Save 15% on your order with code BELIEVE: ⁠https://lagoonsleep.com/pages/lagoon-believe-in-the-run

item.description:

item.title: 159 | Ben Johnson, 4 Marathons in 4 Weeks
item.description:
This spring, Ben Johnson (aka @benjohnson763) ran four marathons in four weeks: Boston, London, Eugene, and Wobegon, averaging a 2:49 pace. He only started running 5 years ago and has since lost a ton of weight and paced Meaghan to some of her personal bests along the way. He's also the true embodiment of Midwest nice and we're lucky to call him a friend. Hear about where he's at with his running, what it was like to run all those races, and what his goals are for 2023 and beyond.


Follow Ben on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/benjohnson763/

This episode is sponsored by Lagoon pillows, we get crazy good sleep with them and you should too. Save 15% on your order with code BELIEVE: ⁠https://lagoonsleep.com/pages/lagoon-believe-in-the-run

item.title: 155 | Ali Feller, Podcast Host (Ali on the Run) and Race Announcer
item.description: Thomas and Meg sit down and chat with Ali Feller, host of the number one running podcast in America (every once in awhile we get there too) and probably also the nicest person in America. She's also a race announcer, recently handling finish line duties at the Boston Marathon. She's also a marathoner. And a mom. And a wife. She's pretty much a transformer but not in disguise. And she just ran a 3:41 marathon PR in Eugene! Hope you enjoy this talk as much as we did.
